text:
There once was a child named Max. He loved inventing. He would spend his nights drawing elaborate machines and gadgets that would make the world a better place. His favorite toy was a tiny engine he crafted with his own hands. It barely made a sound but when it moved, it transported his imagination to futuristic cities.

Max loved exploring his backyard. One day, he created a catapult out of old branches and a tin can. He named it "The Megastomp." When he positioned it, he’d imagine the can as an underground bunker and toss the Megastomp into the air with precision. The can would "blast" down onto a patch of bushes with a satisfying thump, sending leaves flying like rain.

His favorite game involved using The Megastomp to launch model airplanes. He’d create his own models, painting them with bright colors and numbers. The planes would zoom across the sky, chased by imaginary foes or pursued by cute, friendly raccoons that communicated using hand signals
